title: United Equus
content:
United Equus is the first unified world government of the planet [[Equus]], and the first truly interstellar government of the planet. It existed from the reorganization of the Convocation of Creatures into a planetary government in 1104 CYP until the formation of the Solar Empire in 1346 CYP.


United Equus was formed by the emergency resolution to reorganize the mostly diplomatic Convocation of Creatures into a legally binding planetary government following the outbreak of the Great War. Due to the nature of it's formation, there was need for a strong and decisive central government. Even following the end of the Great War and the demilitarization of the economy, the strong central government remained, a definite contrast to the other, decentralized members of the Sector Alliance. Despite this, there remained a high amount of cultural autonomy between the different species, regions, and colonies.

History

Pre-Unification

Prior to the Great War, Equus was made up of a collection of nation states, the most prominent being the Princessdom of Equestria and the Griffon Empire. Equestria became the dominant interstellar power of the planet, although cooperation between nations on interstellar projects was common. The Convocation of Creatures had been founded some many centuries prior to the beginning of Equus' interstellar era, though it mostly served as a diplomatic affair between nations and peoples. Due to rising international cooperation from the dawn of the space age, the Convocation began to take on a more active role in global affairs. Most notably, the committee that oversaw the colonization of Yildun Rest was an arm of the Convocation.

The Great War

The surprise attack on Equus' colony on Yildun Rest in 1104 CYP (2429 CE) was a galvanizing moment of national tragedy for the fledgling interstellar power. The Convocation of Creatures was called into an emergency session immediately after the attack, the assorted nation states unanimously voting to enter a state of war with the Apleen and Huachavi, and after some deliberation, the reorganization of the Convocation into a centralized global government on May 15th, 1104.

Princess Celestia of Equestria was selected as the first President of the Convocation, and in the following month the various systems of governance would be laid into place. Most notably was the formation of Equus High Command and the Equus Military Forces: initially the combined military assets of it's founding states, it would grow to become one of the major militaries of the region during the Great War. Shortly thereafter, the Sector Alliance was created between Equus, the United Systems Confederacy, and the Sarden Tribes, later adding the Preservers to the alliance.

Equus initially faired very poorly in the Great War, it's military was woefully inexperienced and under-equipped compared to their enemies. If not for the efforts and ability of the other members of the Sector Alliance, it is likely Equus would have lost the war almost immediately. Eventually though, the Equus Military Forces was able to grow and bring itself up to a comparable standard to other major interstellar powers. During the first half of the war, most of Equus' resources went into improving the military, including the construction of the ESS Eclipse.

Following the Battle of Caren Prime and the collapse of the Apleen civilian government, Equus was the strongest proponent of peace, believing the war to be essentially won. With the subsequent Zanden Conference and the formation of the Apleen Republic, Equus and the USC declared hostilities in the sector to be at an end, leading to the mutiny of the Continuation Forces and the fracturing of the Sector Alliance.

Although only a relatively small number of Equestrian ships and armies joined the Continuation Forces it was lead and initiated by the the immensely powerful ESS Eclipse and it's CO: the second highest ranking member of the Equus High Command, Admiral Nebula Bolt. The Equestrian Navy was tasked with hunting down and capturing Bolt and the Eclipse, along with the rest of the Continuation Forces, and clashed frequently, although the ESN was never successful in it's goal. Peace had been declared, but the Apleen fleet and Huachavi still considered the war ongoing, and although they primarily fought against the Continuation Forces, there were still many incidents of them fighting against Equestrian Forces.

Eventually, the Continuation Forces were able to destroy the last of the Apleen Fleet and force the Huachavi into surrender, marking the true end of the Great War. All Equestrian members of the Continuation Forces promptly surrendered to Equus, with Admiral Bolt bargaining the pardoning of the Continuation Forces in exchange for him pleading guilty of all charges levied against him. With the reintegration of the Continuation Forces, the Equus Military Forces demobilized for the first time in it's history, and the recovery of the Hydian Sector began.

Reconstruction

Despite the millions of lives lost during the Great War, Equestrian society and economy remained strong following demilitarization. Most reconstruction efforts were focused in the former disputed zone (now fully recognized Equestrian territory), and aid to their new ally, the Apleen Republic. The planet of Yildun Rest in particular, had suffered the complete loss of all infrastructure due to multiple fierce battles over the planet and a prolonged guerilla war.

As part of the early stages of reconstruction, a wave of legal proceedings occurred to legitimize the results of the war, most notably the war crime tribunal of Admiral Nebula Bolt. In a highly controversial ruling, Bolt was allowed to keep his rank and command, and sentence to never be allowed to step foot on Equus again, and would command the Eclipse as a patrol and training vessel in the furthest reaches of Equestrian territory up until it's disappearance in 2450.

As reconstruction waned, a wave of colonization picked up, spurred on by the development of the Las Pegasus II-class colony ship (the Las Pegasus I-class was designed to resettle war torn worlds like Yildun Rest). Many planets, especially in the former disputed zone, were settled during this time, including New Mustangia, Unicornis IV, and Clawrest.

The Hydian Peace

With belligerent powers like of Apleen DepSoc and Huachavi toppled and isolated respectively, the the various interstellar empires of the Hydian Sector entered a long period of prosperity, peace, and slow expansion commonly referred to as the Hydian Peace that lasted from ~2460 to the 2640s. Equus in particular finally became a societal equal to the other species of the area, as life on Equus during the Great War had remained stagnant, staying comparable to late 21st century Earth due to the focus towards the military.

With rapid societal advancement, a golden age of sorts was brought about on Equus, poverty and hunger were completely eliminated during the Hydian Peace, although such issues would rapidly reemerge following later instability. Equus' territory was expanded during this time, with 17 new colonies of various scales established across the stars, and countless mining and research outposts spread throughout.

The population of Equus and it's colonies rapidly expanded during the Hydian Peace; Equus, its moon, and assorted orbital habitats reaching 25 billion inhabitants by 2500. During this period of expansion, Equus incorporated the Sparkle Nebula, a stellar nursery, into its territory. The Sparkle Nebula would prove to be an immense boon to the burgeoning interstellar civilization, as it was extremely rich in natural materials.

The only time during the Hydian Peace that the EMF was mobilized was during the Ichizhou Realms in 2554, which fortunately saw no combat engagements. The crisis was brought on by the territorial expansion of Equus' ally, the Sarden Tribes, and the Sarden's historic rival, the Ichizhou Realms. As border clashes between the two threatened to bring the entire region into war once more, the Equestrian Navy was ordered into Sarden and USC territory along the border with the Ichizhou. The matter was settled peacefully by third party mediation from the Morden Union, which established a neutral zone between Sarden and Ichizhou territory, that prohibited any Sector Alliance or Ichizhou vessel from entering.

Infrastructure Construction

With the raw material of the Sparkle Nebula and the increased economic prosperity from the growing colonies, Equus undertook it's most ambitious project yet. Beginning in 2579, and completed 114 years later in the early years of the Solar Empire, the Olympus Ring was to be a gargantuan artificial ring encircling Equus. It would serve as a massive shipyard, the largest in the sector (the larger Caren Alpha Yards were destroyed during the Great War and wouldn't be rebuilt until after the fall of the Solar Empire). In addition to the shipyard, it would be an orbital habitat for millions of inhabitants, and a star port that facilitated the rapid transport of material to and from the planet's surface.

Most analysts agree that the reckless spending and rampant government corruption that occurred during construction the Olympus Ring is what began the social and political instability that marked the decline of the Hydian Peace for Equus. Frequently behind schedule and over budget, it's plans twisted by various officials and private citizens for their own gain, the incomplete ring above the planet began to draw ire from the general population, who rightfully believed that funds originally intended for social causes had gone into the Ring.

End of the Hydian Peace

Starting in the 2590s, Equus began to enter a period of social, economic, and political decline; spurred on by the construction of the Ring. Social spending was slashed during this time, leading to the reemergence of poverty on Equus, only adding more fuel to the fire. Minor protests flared up on various worlds during the period between 2590 and 2653, until they reached a head with the Puerto Paraíso Scandal. The result of decades of rising government corruption, the scandal began as an instance of simple real-estate fraud on the resort moon of Puerto Paraíso, but investigations quickly uncovered a whole bevy of other, much more sinister crimes that led all the way up the government ladder. The current president, Gucharo Picorroca, was found guilty in multiple crimes related to the scandal, becoming the only President of the Convocation to ever be imprisoned. Following Picorroca's trial, his entire cabinet, along with his successor, and 56 members of the Convocation all resigned their posts in a last ditch attempt to avoid justice.

In an attempt to restore faith in the government, Convocation member Princess Celestia was selected as the interim president. Celestia had routinely been an immensely popular figure; Prior to her appointment as interim president, she had served 15 consecutive and non-consecutive terms as President. Celestia was properly elected president shortly thereafter, winning a landslide victory the likes of which hadn't been seen since the Great War.

Despite Celestia's crusade against government corruption, tensions still remained high, as she was unable to fully alleviate the socio-economic issues that had begun to show up in the prior decades. In addition to the social issues, external problems were rampant during the 2630s. Due to the ecological collapse on the Janset colony brought about by overhunting of native fauna, multiple Sarden Tribes took to a life of piracy, primarily raiding into Equestrian Space. Additionally, the Huachavi finally ended their long period of isolationism and began to antagonize the Sector Alliance through subversive means.

All the issues brewing under the surface of Equestrian society reached a head in 2666 with the Assassination of Princess Cadence. The then President Celestia took the death of her neice hard, and began pushing harder and harder to find the perpetrators of the assassination. Any suspects were rounded up and held without trial, alongside many more gross violations of the rule of law. Eventually, the investigation determined that the attack was carried out by Huachavi operatives with the blessing of the Huachavi government.

Equus immediately called for war against the Huachavi, but the other members of the Sector Alliance refused to join, citing the multitude of holes in the investigation's finding. Because of this perceived betrayal, Equus formally exited the Sector Alliance in 2668. They would be followed by the fractured Sarden Tribes and the Preservers in 2669 and 2670 respectively, leaving only the USC and the Apleen Republic.

Instability and Reformation

Following the exit from the Sector Alliance, xenophobia on Equus and her colonies ran rampant, and violent hate crimes against aliens - especially against Huachavi - were common. In March of 2670, the Manehattan Race Riots broke out in Equus' most diverse city, and grew to such a scale the necessitated the deployment of the Equus Planetary Forces. During the riots, Celestia was granted emergency powers in order to bring order to the city, and to prevent any other large scale protests. The rioters in Manehattan were crushed, but the Convocation didn't bother to end Celestia's emergency powers, instead extending the state of emergency until Celestia herself saw fit to declare it over.

A year later, in 2671, a reports of a skirmish above the Equestrian Nekmer colony (A system that had been annexed from the Huachavi at the conclusion of the Great War) took Equus in a frenzy. This so-called "Battle of Nekmer" supposedly saw a small Huachavi task force enter the system in an attempt to occupy the colony, but were beaten back by the brave Equestrian defenders. To date, no concrete evidence that any Huachevi armed assets were present or even near the Nekmer system at the recorded date of the battle - it is widely believed by modern scholars that the "Battle" was a either false flag attack orchestrated covertly by the Equus military, or a complete fabrication by Celestia's government.

Nonetheless, the public outcry over the precieved attack turned against the last remaining opposition to Celestia's rule. Those that didn't support her were blamed for "allowing" the attack to happen, and any remaining opposition Convocation members were purged from the body. Through it all, Celestia was increasingly viewed as the guilding beacon of stability that kept Equus safe, until the Convocation (now completely controlled by the pro-Celestia camp) declared her the "Solar Empress" of Equus, and formalized her emergency powers into complete autocracy. Following the proclamation of the Solar Empire, the Convocation disbanded itself permanently, completely passing all governmental power to the newly christend Empress Celestia.
